On this page

Mobile Technology ​

Mobile technology has transformed how we communicate, work, learn, and entertain ourselves. A device in your pocket has more computing power than the computers that landed humans on the moon — and it is always connected.

Grade 12

Mobile technology is primarily a Grade 12 topic, though mobile devices are introduced in Grade 10 as a type of computer. Grade 12 focus: advantages/limitations of mobile, 4G/5G, mobile OS, mobile security.

What is Mobile Technology? ​

Mobile technology refers to portable computing devices and the wireless networks that connect them, enabling communication and computing from any location.

Mobile Devices ​

DeviceDescriptionTypical OS
SmartphonePhone with advanced computing (apps, internet, camera, GPS)Android, iOS
Feature phoneBasic phone — calls and SMS only, no appsProprietary OS
TabletLarger touchscreen device, no built-in phoneAndroid, iPadOS, Windows
SmartwatchWrist-worn computer — health tracking, notificationsWear OS, watchOS
Fitness trackerWearable measuring steps, heart rate, sleepProprietary
LaptopPortable full computerWindows, macOS, Linux
eReaderSpecialised for reading e-booksCustom (Kindle)

Mobile Operating Systems ​

Android (Google) ​

  • Open source — manufacturers can customise it
  • Used by Samsung, Huawei, Xiaomi, and many others
  • Largest market share globally
  • Apps from Google Play Store
  • More customisable; slightly more malware risk due to sideloading

iOS (Apple) ​

  • Closed source — only on Apple iPhones and iPads
  • Tightly controlled ecosystem
  • Apps only from Apple App Store (strict approval process)
  • Generally more secure and consistent updates
  • Less customisable

Comparison ​

FeatureAndroidiOS
ManufacturerMultiple (Samsung, Huawei, etc.)Apple only
SourceOpen sourceClosed source
App storeGoogle PlayApple App Store
CustomisationHighLimited
Market share~72% global~27% global
UpdatesVaries by manufacturerGuaranteed for years
Price rangeBudget to premiumPremium only

Mobile Network Generations ​

GenerationSpeedLatencyKey Feature
2G (GSM)~0.1 MbpsHighVoice calls + SMS
3G~2–42 Mbps~100msMobile internet
4G LTE~10–150 Mbps~30–50msHD video, mobile apps
5G~100 Mbps–10 Gbps<10msIoT, autonomous vehicles, AR/VR

4G vs 5G ​

Feature4G LTE5G
Max speed~150 Mbps~10 Gbps
Latency30–50 ms<1 ms
CapacityModerate densityVery high device density
InfrastructureExisting towersNew mmWave towers + existing
CoverageWidespreadStill expanding (2024)
Use casesStreaming, browsingAutonomous vehicles, smart cities, AR

Advantages of Mobile Computing ​

AdvantageExplanation
PortabilityWork, communicate, access data from anywhere
Always connected4G/5G/Wi-Fi — constant internet access
GPS and locationNavigation, location-based services
Integrated cameraPhotography, video, document scanning
Health monitoringSteps, heart rate, sleep tracking (wearables)
Cloud syncData backed up and accessible across all devices
App ecosystemMillions of apps for every purpose
Contactless paymentApple Pay, Google Pay — pay without a card

Disadvantages and Limitations of Mobile Computing ​

LimitationExplanation
Battery lifePowerful features drain battery; constant charging needed
Small screenLimits usability for complex tasks
Input methodTouchscreen typing is slower than physical keyboard
Processing powerLess powerful than desktop/laptop for same price
StorageInternal storage often limited (mitigated by cloud)
Security risksPhysical theft; malware apps; public Wi-Fi interception
Connectivity dependenceMany features unusable without internet
Health concernsEye strain, posture, radiation (minimal but debated)
DistractionNotifications reduce focus and productivity

Mobile Security Threats ​

ThreatDescription
Device theftPhysical loss of device gives attacker access to data
Malicious appsApps that contain malware (especially from unofficial sources)
Public Wi-FiUnencrypted connections can be intercepted
Phishing via SMS"Smishing" — fake text messages with malicious links
SpywareApps that secretly track location, messages, calls
RansomwareIncreasingly targeting mobile devices

Mobile Security Best Practices ​

  • Use a strong PIN, password, or biometric lock
  • Only install apps from official stores (Play Store / App Store)
  • Keep OS and apps updated
  • Use VPN on public Wi-Fi
  • Enable remote wipe (Find My iPhone / Find My Device)
  • Be cautious of app permissions — does a calculator need access to contacts?

Mobile vs Desktop for Different Tasks ​

TaskBetter on MobileBetter on Desktop
Social media✓
Navigation/GPS✓
Photography✓
Video editing✓
Coding✓
Spreadsheet work✓
Quick communication✓
Long-form writing✓

Internet of Things (IoT) ​

IoT is the network of physical objects embedded with sensors, software, and connectivity that allows them to collect and exchange data.

Examples:

  • Smart home devices (lights, thermostat, locks)
  • Smart city traffic management
  • Wearable health monitors
  • Industrial sensors in factories
  • Smart agriculture (soil moisture sensors)

IoT devices connect via Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Zigbee, or 5G. 5G's low latency and high device density make it ideal for IoT.

Key Terms ​

TermDefinition
Mobile OSOperating system designed for touch-screen portable devices
AndroidOpen-source mobile OS by Google
iOSApple's closed-source mobile OS
4G/LTE4th generation mobile network — fast mobile internet
5G5th generation network — ultra-fast, ultra-low latency
LatencyDelay between sending a request and receiving a response
IoTInternet of Things — connected physical devices
SmishingSMS phishing — scam text messages
WearableComputer worn on body (watch, fitness band)
Cloud syncAutomatic copying of data to cloud storage

Exam Focus ​

High-Frequency Questions

  1. "Give THREE advantages of mobile computing" — portability, always connected, GPS, camera, cloud sync — always explain, don't just list

  2. "Give THREE limitations/disadvantages of mobile computing" — battery life, small screen, security risks, processing power, connectivity dependence

  3. "Compare Android and iOS" — open source vs closed; multiple manufacturers vs Apple only; customisation; app store control

  4. "Compare 4G and 5G" — speed (5G much faster), latency (5G <10ms vs 4G 30–50ms), coverage (4G wider), use cases (5G enables IoT, autonomous vehicles)

  5. "What is IoT? Give TWO examples." — network of physical objects with sensors and connectivity; examples: smart home devices, wearable health monitors, industrial sensors
